raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find .php recursively, wc -l each, sum. Use find and awk. Command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | awk 'END{print "Total:", $1}'? Actually wc -l prints count filename. We want each line count and total. Use awk to sum. Command: find /testbed -type f -name '*.php' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1} END{print "Total:", sum}'. But need to print each file count too. wc -l already prints. So just pipe to awk to sum. Use: find ... | xargs -0 wc -l | awk 'NR>1{sum+=$1} END{print "Total:", sum}'. That prints each line count and total. Provide command.
